jokes.text: All right, all right. Don't tell me. A rabbit.
jokes.setup: Someone asks others to guess what's in a box/container
jokes.punchline: Will immediately guesses correctly with deadpan confidence
jokes.explanation: Classic Will deadpan delivery, very economical setup/punchline

jokes.text: Yeah. I'm really good at this. Ha-ha.
jokes.setup: Will guesses correctly on obvious rabbit reveal
jokes.punchline: Self-congratulatory comment about his 'amazing' deductive skills
jokes.explanation: Standard Will swagger, moderately funny follow-up

jokes.text: Aww. Oh, look at the little lumpy bumpy bunny wunny.
jokes.setup: Adult woman sees a rabbit
jokes.punchline: Hilary's over-the-top baby talk with ridiculous alliterative description
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary character moment - vapid but endearing baby talk

jokes.text: I remember. That was the Easter Grandma Hattie came over... and made that secret stew. What was that?
jokes.setup: Carlton tells innocent story about his disappeared rabbit
jokes.punchline: Will connects it to mysterious stew made by grandmother
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark implication that Grandma cooked the rabbit, excellent misdirection

jokes.text: It tasted like chicken but it wasn't.
jokes.setup: Previous setup about mysterious stew
jokes.punchline: Classic 'tastes like chicken' line heavily implying rabbit meat
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and classic 'tastes like chicken' reference with dark twist

jokes.text: Oh, my God. I remember that stew. It was delicious.
jokes.setup: Realization that the stew was likely made from Carlton's pet rabbit
jokes.punchline: Hilary fondly remembers how delicious it was
jokes.explanation: Classic Hilary obliviousness to the dark implications

jokes.text: Geoffrey, you gotta make that stew. Don't tempt me.
jokes.setup: Hilary wants Geoffrey to recreate the mysterious rabbit stew
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's dry 'don't tempt me' suggests he knows exactly what it was
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's dry wit caps the rabbit stew sequence perfectly

jokes.text: Tell old pharaoh Let my cousin grow
jokes.setup: Carlton practicing traditional spiritual 'Let My People Go'
jokes.punchline: Mishears/changes lyrics to nonsensical 'Let my cousin grow'
jokes.explanation: Mild Carlton earnestness humor with lyric misunderstanding

jokes.text: Would you look at that? Shameful sucking up.
jokes.setup: Ashley compliments the Reverend on his golf game and shirt
jokes.punchline: Will calls out Ashley's obvious brown-nosing
jokes.explanation: Direct observation humor, decent economy

jokes.text: Great, now I'm gonna have to buy another sweater.
jokes.setup: Carlton learns the Reverend doesn't make the final decision about the solo
jokes.punchline: Carlton realizes he wasted money bribing the wrong person
jokes.explanation: Good Carlton character moment about his scheming backfiring

jokes.text: Oh, I be in the back.
jokes.setup: Reverend says he hasn't seen Will at church lately
jokes.punchline: Will's excuse that he sits in the back (where he can't be seen)
jokes.explanation: Good Will deflection humor with economical delivery

jokes.text: Oh. Yeah, Rev, you know, Sunday's kind of a bad day for me, you know... what with Saturday usually being a late-night night... you know what I'm talking about? But I tell you what, y'all get something kicked off on a Tuesday, I'm there.
jokes.setup: Reverend suggests Will should come to church more
jokes.punchline: Will's elaborate excuse about Saturday nights making Sunday bad, offers Tuesday instead
jokes.explanation: Classic Will rambling excuse with good character specificity

jokes.text: Tomorrow, gosh. Man. You know, any other day but tomorrow I could've done it, you know. Even yesterday. Even yesterday. But... Pbbt.
jokes.setup: Reverend asks Will to help decorate tomorrow
jokes.punchline: Will's ridiculous excuse that yesterday would have been perfect but tomorrow is impossible
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic - yesterday is somehow more feasible than tomorrow

jokes.text: Ladies? There's gonna be some honeys up in there? I can guarantee at least a couple honeys. Heh. I'll see you tomorrow.
jokes.setup: Will has been making elaborate excuses to avoid church work
jokes.punchline: The mention of ladies immediately changes his mind
jokes.explanation: Good character-driven misdirection showing Will's priorities

jokes.text: Come on down there and I get myself a little sticky, you know what I'm talking... You know. You know what I'm saying. You know, because honey is sticky. You know.
jokes.setup: Will is excited about meeting 'honeys' at church
jokes.punchline: Awkward attempt to make a honey/sticky pun that he has to over-explain
jokes.explanation: Weak pun that's over-explained, but the awkwardness might be intentional

jokes.text: My, what a handsome devil. And I feel kind of devilish.
jokes.setup: Elderly church lady sees young Will arrive to help
jokes.punchline: Suggestive comment from sweet old church lady
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of sweet church lady expectations

jokes.text: Whoo! Oh! Oh. Oh, man. Oh, I got a page. Oh, it's 911. Tupac just got arrested again. I need to go.
jokes.setup: Will is clearly uncomfortable with aggressive elderly ladies
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous excuse about Tupac getting arrested (as if Will would be personally involved)
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist escape excuse with good 90s cultural reference

jokes.text: I didn't hear any pager. Oh, um... Well, this is the kind that vibrates. I didn't see your hip vibrating. Wish I had though.
jokes.setup: Will tries to use fake pager excuse to escape
jokes.punchline: Elderly lady calls him out, then makes suggestive comment about his hips
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of inappropriate church lady behavior

jokes.text: Not too bad. Except for all them old ladies down there pinching my cheeks. Well, Will, that's the way old ladies are. They see a young man with a cute face, they love to pinch his cheeks. I ain't talking about my face.
jokes.setup: Phil explains old ladies pinch cheeks of cute young men on their faces
jokes.punchline: Will clarifies they're not pinching his face cheeks
jokes.explanation: Classic setup/punchline misdirection with perfect timing

jokes.text: Yeah, unless Grandma Hattie pulls another Fatal Attraction.
jokes.setup: Nicky says he'll keep his rabbit Harry forever
jokes.punchline: Reference back to Grandma Hattie cooking Carlton's rabbit
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Fatal Attraction reference for the rabbit cooking

jokes.text: You gotta teach him, like, some aerabbits. Rock, rock the rabbit, rock Don't stop
jokes.setup: Will suggests the rabbit needs exercise
jokes.punchline: Aerobics/aerabbits pun followed by rap-style chant
jokes.explanation: Decent pun with Will's characteristic rap delivery style

jokes.text: Hey, stop playing, Verna. Ain't nobody that nearsighted.
jokes.setup: Elderly church lady Verna presumably doing something flirtatious
jokes.punchline: Will calls out her obvious fake excuse for getting close
jokes.explanation: Decent observation about obvious fake excuse for physical contact

jokes.text: Your cookies suck. Well, suck this.
jokes.setup: Elderly church ladies competing over whose cookies Will prefers
jokes.punchline: Will's blunt honesty leads to aggressive response from Rose
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation, economical but not particularly clever

jokes.text: You know, it does feel good to give something back to the communit... Hey.
jokes.setup: Will gives sincere speech about community service
jokes.punchline: Gets interrupted mid-word, presumably by falling or physical mishap
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy interrupting a sincere moment

jokes.text: Visual gag - Will falls off ladder during his speech about giving back
jokes.setup: Will gives noble speech about giving back to the community
jokes.punchline: Immediately falls off ladder he's on, undermining his noble moment
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy timing - undercutting sincere moment with pratfall

jokes.text: Nice try. Now watch how it's done. Make a note. Unruly.
jokes.setup: Ashley finishes a nice rendition of 'Amazing Grace'
jokes.punchline: Carlton dismisses it and declares her 'unruly' before his performance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton competitiveness and prissy judgment

jokes.text: Vivian, I've always been in love with you.
jokes.setup: Reverend and Vivian having innocent conversation about church work
jokes.punchline: Sudden inappropriate declaration of love
jokes.explanation: Standard awkward romantic confession setup for episode conflict

jokes.text: Well, first of all, because you seasoning the chicken with cleanser.
jokes.setup: Will asks Vivian if she's okay, she says she's fine
jokes.punchline: Evidence she's distracted - using cleaning product instead of seasoning
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy showing Vivian's distracted state

jokes.text: Makes sense to me. Look like the Rev's trying to get his hallelujah on.
jokes.setup: Vivian says the Reverend's confession doesn't make sense
jokes.punchline: Will's slang interpretation with religious pun
jokes.explanation: Good Will-style religious wordplay with characteristic slang

jokes.text: I was asking you what's going on because there ain't nothing going on in here. There's nothing going... I wanted to know with you. Did you have something going on? Because we don't have any.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil enters cheerfully asking what's going on
jokes.punchline: Will's nervous over-explanation trying to cover up the awkward situation
jokes.explanation: Good nervous rambling but lacks economy and clarity

jokes.text: You can't. Uh... Ain't enough chicken. I mean, there's too much chicken. You know what they say about ministers. You know, they will eat and eat and eat until they die. Yeah. They're like goldfish.
jokes.setup: Phil wants to invite Reverend for dinner
jokes.punchline: Will's increasingly desperate excuses culminating in ministers being like goldfish
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist escalation with memorable goldfish comparison

jokes.text: Come on, Harry, give me another one. Be all that you can be
jokes.setup: Nicky is exercising his rabbit Harry
jokes.punchline: Uses Army recruitment slogan to motivate a rabbit
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from kid using Army slogan for pet training

jokes.text: Where's Harry? Uh... He went upstairs, boom, two steps at a time. I guess he was really hungry. Where's his box? He took it with him. Because he knew it didn't belong down here, he put it away.
jokes.setup: Nicky looking for his missing rabbit Harry
jokes.punchline: Phil's increasingly desperate lies about the rabbit's whereabouts and capabilities
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy from Phil's obvious lies getting more elaborate

jokes.text: But if he was hungry, why didn't he just come in the kitchen? Nicky, he's just a rabbit.
jokes.setup: Phil claims rabbit went upstairs because he was hungry
jokes.punchline: Kid's logical question exposes the absurdity; Phil's weak deflection
jokes.explanation: Good kid logic exposing adult's weak cover-up story

jokes.text: What? What? Uncle Phil, you killed him? Oh, my God. Does anybody know?
jokes.setup: Phil says something terrible happened and he didn't mean to kill 'him'
jokes.punchline: Will assumes Phil killed a person, not a rabbit
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection playing on ambiguous pronoun reference

jokes.text: Okay. All right. All right. Uncle Phil, go upstairs, pack a bag. I'm gonna get the car. We gonna shoot down to Tijuana. We gonna chill in Mexico, this whole thing blows over.
jokes.setup: Will thinks Uncle Phil committed murder
jokes.punchline: Will immediately jumps to elaborate escape plan to Mexico
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Will immediately ready to help family flee the country

jokes.text: Will, they don't arrest you for sitting on a rabbit.
jokes.setup: Elaborate murder cover-up discussion
jokes.punchline: Phil's matter-of-fact clarification about rabbit death
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan revelation that deflates all the murder tension

jokes.text: I thought you killed Harry. Will, who did you think I killed? I thought you killed Harry.
jokes.setup: Confusion about who Phil killed
jokes.punchline: Will thought Phil killed Harry (a person) not Harry (the rabbit)
jokes.explanation: Clarifying the misdirection but somewhat redundant at this point

jokes.text: It was all they had.
jokes.setup: Phil asked Hilary to get a replacement rabbit that looks like Harry
jokes.punchline: Hilary's deadpan excuse for bringing back obviously wrong replacement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary response - completely unhelpful but delivered with confidence

jokes.text: Would you prefer a breast or thigh? Uh! He'll take a wing.
jokes.setup: Vivian innocently asks Reverend about chicken preference
jokes.punchline: Will jumps in to prevent any suggestive conversation
jokes.explanation: Standard sexual innuendo avoidance, functional but predictable

jokes.text: Could you pass the pork... dork?
jokes.setup: Carlton trying to insult the Reverend at dinner
jokes.punchline: Weak attempt at insult by rhyming 'pork' with 'dork'
jokes.explanation: Weak insult but perfectly fits Carlton's character - trying to be mean but failing

jokes.text: Yeah, you might wanna cover all the commandments this year.
jokes.setup: Will comments on Reverend's upcoming Easter sermon
jokes.punchline: Suggesting the Reverend needs to review the commandments (implying adultery)
jokes.explanation: Sharp, economical dig at the Reverend's moral failing

jokes.text: Daddy, you know how you told me that Harry's black now... because rabbits change colors for Easter? Isn't there a little more to that story?
jokes.setup: Phil tried to explain replacement rabbit with absurd color-change story
jokes.punchline: Kid's innocent but knowing question about the obvious lie
jokes.explanation: Good kid's wisdom seeing through adult's obvious lie

jokes.text: Daddy, did you sit on Harry? Yes. But... But... That's okay, Dad. Death is a natural part of life. But what a way to go.
jokes.setup: Kid confronts Phil about killing the rabbit
jokes.punchline: Wise acceptance of death followed by commentary on the method
jokes.explanation: Great kid wisdom followed by perfectly timed dark humor about Phil's size
